deep_dive.title: Sequential Correlations Change In-Context Learning: Effective Context Length and Architectural Mismatch
deep_dive.abstract: Modern sequence models have a striking capacity for in-context learning (ICL); they can perform new tasks based only on examples given in the prompt. Understanding how this ability emerges requires theory that captures important properties of natural data. Linear regression has served as a useful sandbox for ICL theory, but existing work has largely focused on prompts with independent examples. In this work, we extend this setting to sequentially correlated data, a basic feature of real sequences. We present a solvable model based on linear attention and test our predictions on realistic transformer architectures. We identify two distinct effects: First, when the query token is independent of the context, within-context correlations induce an effective context length: correlated prompts behave like shorter i.i.d. prompts. Second, when the query is also correlated with its context, test error is reduced, particularly for softmax attention when compared to linear attention. These results suggest that correlated prompts alter not only the effective sample size of in-context learning, but also which attention architectures are best matched to the task.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: solvable model based on linear attention for in-context learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于线性注意力的可解模型用于上下文学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于线性注意力的可解模型用于上下文学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于线性注意力的可解模型用于上下文学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents a solvable linear attention model to study in-context learning with sequential correlations, revealing changes in effective context length and architectural mismatch.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的上下文学习理论多假设提示样本独立，不符合真实数据特性。本文提出基于线性注意力的可解模型，处理序列相关数据，并在真实Transformer上进行验证。发现当查询与上下文独立时，有效上下文长度会扩展；而序列相关性会导致架构失配。该工作为理解线性注意力在序列数据上的行为提供了理论基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有的上下文学习理论多假设提示样本独立，不符合真实数据特性。本文提出基于线性注意力的可解模型，处理序列相关数据，并在真实Transformer上进行验证。发现当查询与上下文独立时，有效上下文长度会扩展；而序列相关性会导致架构失配。该工作为理解线性注意力在序列数据上的行为提供了理论基础。

deep_dive.title: Think Big, Search Small: Where Capacity Matters in Hierarchical Search Agents?
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model based search agents increasingly adopt multi-agent architectures in which a main agent decomposes a complex question into sub-queries and dispatches them to parallel sub-agents. However, existing systems instantiate all roles from a single model of identical scale, leaving open how model capacity should be distributed across roles. We factorize hierarchical search into three roles: a delegation role responsible for task decomposition, an execution role responsible for retrieval and evidence extraction, and an answer generation role held fixed as a confound control. We then conduct controlled capacity sweeps along the delegation and execution axes on five multi-hop QA benchmarks. The experiments yield three findings. First, role factorization consistently outperforms a single-agent baseline, improving exact match from 4.5 to 8.6 points across six model scales. Second, capacity sensitivity is asymmetric: scaling the delegation backbone improves EM by ~11 points, whereas scaling the execution sub-agent moves EM by only ~2.6 points, identifying decomposition as the capability bottleneck. Third, a 1.7B-parameter executor trained via quality-filtered trajectory distillation matches a frontier sub-agent in accuracy while consuming 37% fewer sub-agent tokens, advancing the Pareto frontier. These results suggest a concrete recipe for building hierarchical search agents: concentrate capacity at delegation and downsize execution without sacrificing accuracy. Our code is available at https://github.com/QinnanCai0115/role-factorized-search.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: studies multi-agent architecture for hierarchical search with delegation and execution roles
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究了层次搜索中多代理架构，包含分解和执行角色
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究了层次搜索中多代理架构，包含分解和执行角色
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究了层次搜索中多代理架构，包含分解和执行角色
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Investigates how model capacity distribution across roles in hierarchical multi-agent search agents affects performance on multi-hop QA, revealing that delegation capacity matters more.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有搜索代理采用多代理架构但所有角色使用相同规模模型。本文分解层次搜索为委派、执行和生成角色，通过控制容量扫描发现委派角色的模型容量对最终性能影响最大。实验在五个多跳QA基准上进行，为多代理系统的资源分配提供了关键见解。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有搜索代理采用多代理架构但所有角色使用相同规模模型。本文分解层次搜索为委派、执行和生成角色，通过控制容量扫描发现委派角色的模型容量对最终性能影响最大。实验在五个多跳QA基准上进行，为多代理系统的资源分配提供了关键见解。

deep_dive.title: Who Broke the System? Failure Localization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM) based multi-agent systems enable complex problem solving through coordinated reasoning and action, but their distributed structure also introduces new challenges in diagnosing system-level failures. When an execution fails, identifying which agent is responsible and at what point the trajectory first becomes irreversibly misdirected is difficult due to long-horizon interactions and tightly coupled agent behaviors. In this paper, we study the problem of failure localization in LLM-based multi-agent systems and present AgentLocate, a framework that attributes failures to both a specific agent and the earliest decisive step. AgentLocate combines an LLM-based judging mechanism with multi-perspective verification by independent evaluators, whose assessments are aggregated using a confidence-aware strategy. The resulting feedback is further used to adapt the judge through lightweight fine-tuning, improving attribution quality. We evaluate AgentLocate on two complementary benchmarks covering diverse tasks, agent configurations, and trajectory lengths. Experimental results show that AgentLocate consistently outperforms existing failure localization methods in identifying both responsible agents and failure steps, while remaining efficient in terms of token usage and running time.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent interaction dynamics and failure localization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体交互动力学与故障定位
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多智能体交互动力学与故障定位
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多智能体交互动力学与故障定位
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AgentLocate to attribute failures in LLM-based multi-agent systems to specific agents and decisive steps, enabling diagnosis of tightly coupled interactions. It combines LLM judging with multi-perspective verification for effective localization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 在基于LLM的多智能体系统中，由于智能体行为长期耦合，系统故障难以定位至具体智能体或步骤。本文提出AgentLocate框架，利用LLM判断机制与多视角独立验证，将故障归因到特定智能体及其最早导致不可逆错误的关键步骤。实验证明该方法能有效识别复杂交互中的故障根源。该工作为提升多智能体系统的可诊断性和可靠性提供了实用工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 在基于LLM的多智能体系统中，由于智能体行为长期耦合，系统故障难以定位至具体智能体或步骤。本文提出AgentLocate框架，利用LLM判断机制与多视角独立验证，将故障归因到特定智能体及其最早导致不可逆错误的关键步骤。实验证明该方法能有效识别复杂交互中的故障根源。该工作为提升多智能体系统的可诊断性和可靠性提供了实用工具。

quick_skim.title: Swarm-Driven Multi-Agent Reasoning for Smart City Security
quick_skim.abstract: Modern smart cities are interconnected cyber-physical ecosystems where heterogeneous devices exchange data and control commands. Coordinated attacks may appear as weak and distributed indicators, including low-rate scanning, abnormal credential use, protocol misuse, or delayed lateral movement, with each signal remaining below local alert thresholds. Therefore, smart-city security is not only an anomaly detection task but also a reasoning task under uncertainty, partial observability, and adversarial manipulation. This work presents TPSC-Sec, an LLM-based multi-agent approach for stable security reasoning in smart cities. TPSC-Sec decomposes analysis across specialized agents that inspect traffic behavior, protocol interactions, identity usage, and temporal attack progression. Their independent threat hypotheses are aggregated by the proposed Threat-Pheromone Swarm Consensus mechanism, which reinforces supported hypotheses, suppresses contradictions, and preserves temporal consistency, thereby driving competing interpretations toward a stable collective decision. We further introduce Adaptive Verified TPSC, which adds verification-aware calibration, context-sensitive weighting, and disagreement-adaptive control to reduce unsupported LLM outputs and reasoning inconsistency. Experiments over 500 runs show that TPSC-Sec achieves a high consensus acceptance rate of 0.97 plus or minus 0.02, hypothesis-support concentration above 0.99, a consensus margin of 2.08 plus or minus 0.21, low aggregate risk of 0.23 plus or minus 0.04, high inter-agent agreement of 0.82 plus or minus 0.06, and strong support-quality correlation of r equals 0.93. Adaptive agent selection reduces the number of active agents by 50 percent while improving system fitness by 11.6 percent. These results demonstrate robust, interpretable, and efficient security reasoning for adversary-resilient smart-city environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: swarm-driven multi-agent reasoning for interaction dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 群体驱动的多智能体推理，涉及交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 群体驱动的多智能体推理，涉及交互动力学
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 群体驱动的多智能体推理，涉及交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TPSC-Sec uses LLM-based multi-agent reasoning to detect coordinated attacks in smart cities through specialized agents inspecting traffic, protocol, identity, and temporal patterns.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 智慧城市中异构设备易受分布式低速率攻击，单个信号低于局部阈值。TPSC-Sec提出基于LLM的多智能体方法，分解分析任务到专门智能体：检查流量行为、协议交互、身份使用和时间攻击模式。这些智能体通过协作推理，综合弱信号识别出潜在威胁。实验表明，该方法在合成攻击场景中显著优于单智能体基线，有效处理了不完整和对抗性观测下的安全推理。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 智慧城市中异构设备易受分布式低速率攻击，单个信号低于局部阈值。TPSC-Sec提出基于LLM的多智能体方法，分解分析任务到专门智能体：检查流量行为、协议交互、身份使用和时间攻击模式。这些智能体通过协作推理，综合弱信号识别出潜在威胁。实验表明，该方法在合成攻击场景中显著优于单智能体基线，有效处理了不完整和对抗性观测下的安全推理。

quick_skim.title: Social Networks of LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interacting populations, raising the question of what such populations come to believe collectively. Whether a population aggregates genuine knowledge or collapses into a false consensus directly affects how much such systems can be trusted. Classical social-network models assume that the network itself determines how beliefs combine. This assumption breaks down for LLM agents, whose limited attention takes in only part of what they are exposed to, so these models overstate how much information a population actually pools and cannot tell genuine consensus from herding. We introduce SNLA, a framework that models how much each agent actually influences others, rather than merely how the network connects them. This influence depends on each agent's position in the network and on how sharply attention focuses. Theoretically, we show on a tractable proxy that narrow attention causes herding, where the effective sample size stays bounded regardless of population size, while wide attention recovers wisdom-of-crowds behavior only when the exposure graph is undirected and degree-regular. Empirically, a controlled testbed validates these predictions directly, and the herding-wisdom transition reproduces on operator-controlled variants of three multi-agent LLM benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent social network dynamics and influence
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体社交网络动态与影响力传播
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体社交网络动态与影响力传播
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体社交网络动态与影响力传播
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SNLA framework to model influence between LLM agents in social networks, accounting for limited attention and avoiding overestimation of information pooling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM智能体在社交网络中可能产生虚假共识的问题，提出SNLA框架，通过建模每个智能体对其他智能体的实际影响力（而非仅网络连接），避免了经典模型高估信息汇聚的缺陷。实验表明该框架能更好区分真正共识与羊群效应。该工作为理解LLM群体行为提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对LLM智能体在社交网络中可能产生虚假共识的问题，提出SNLA框架，通过建模每个智能体对其他智能体的实际影响力（而非仅网络连接），避免了经典模型高估信息汇聚的缺陷。实验表明该框架能更好区分真正共识与羊群效应。该工作为理解LLM群体行为提供了新工具。

quick_skim.title: Multi-Robot Open Adaptive Teaming Across Unseen Environments, Partners, and Scales
quick_skim.abstract: Deploying robot teams in the real world requires simultaneous adaptation to unseen environments, unknown partners, and varying team sizes, yet existing approaches often address these challenges in isolation under the closed-world assumption of fixed teammates. We formalize this as open adaptive multi-robot teaming and propose a hypergraphic-form game formulation that captures team-level cooperative relationships beyond pairwise interactions, providing a principled foundation for coordination structure inference when team composition changes dynamically within episodes. Unlike graph neural network architectures, this is a game-theoretic construct for modeling strategic interactions and payoff structures among agents. Building on this formulation, we develop the Hypergraphic Open-ended Learning Algorithm (HOLA), which progressively expands partner and environment diversity during training rather than optimizing for fixed configurations. Evaluated on cooperative pursuit with multi-drone and multi-quadruped platforms, HOLA outperforms all baselines across all three adaptability dimensions. Learned policies transfer directly to physical hardware without fine-tuning, with successful deployments on Crazyflie and Zsibot L1 platforms confirming robust real-world coordination in novel environments with unseen teammates.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-robot teaming and adaptive cooperation dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多机器人团队与自适应合作动力学
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多机器人团队与自适应合作动力学
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多机器人团队与自适应合作动力学
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Formalizes open adaptive multi-robot teaming and proposes a hypergraphic-form game to capture cooperative relationships beyond pairwise interactions, enabling adaptation to dynamic team composition.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对真实世界中多机器人团队需同时适应未知环境、队友和队形变化的问题，本文形式化定义了开放自适应多机器人团队问题，并提出超图博弈模型以捕获超越成对合作的团队级协同关系。该方法为动态团队中协调结构推断提供理论框架。实验验证了模型在变化环境中的适应能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对真实世界中多机器人团队需同时适应未知环境、队友和队形变化的问题，本文形式化定义了开放自适应多机器人团队问题，并提出超图博弈模型以捕获超越成对合作的团队级协同关系。该方法为动态团队中协调结构推断提供理论框架。实验验证了模型在变化环境中的适应能力。

quick_skim.title: ProACT: Towards Breakdown-Aware Proactive Agent in Multi-User Collaboration
quick_skim.abstract: Conversational agents are increasingly embedded in human collaborative work, yet they remain fundamentally passive and reactive: they respond to explicit user requests rather than proactively recognizing moments when a team would benefit from timely intervention as human collaborators often do. This reactive design substantially limits the use of agents as active participants in multi-user collaboration, where disagreements, ambiguous goals, forgotten constraints, underspecified plans, discussion loops, and imbalanced participation can gradually undermine group progress. To move agents from passive assistants toward active participants in multi-user collaboration, we introduce ProACT, a breakdown-aware agent framework grounded in theories of common ground, collaborative planning, and coordination work. ProACT observes the speaker-attributed conversation history, determines whether the current turn contains a collaboration breakdown requiring intervention, decides whether the agent should stay silent or speak, and, when speaking is needed, routes the case to a targeted collaboration skill. We further introduce the first multi-user collaboration benchmark for evaluating proactive agents across project planning, product design, research collaboration, logistics, education, and resource-constrained decision making. Across 3,244 turn-level examples and five LLM backbones, ProACT consistently improves collaborative appropriateness, non-interruptiveness, conciseness, and judged intervention quality over direct chat.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proactive agent in multi-user collaboration dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多用户协作中的主动智能体，涉及交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多用户协作中的主动智能体，涉及交互动力学
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多用户协作中的主动智能体，涉及交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ProACT enables proactive intervention in multi-user collaboration by detecting breakdowns like disagreements, ambiguous goals, and forgotten constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 对话智能体在多用户协作中通常被动响应，无法像人类一样主动干预。ProACT基于常识沟通理论，构建故障感知代理框架，能够识别团队中的分歧、模糊目标、遗忘约束等障碍，并在适当时机主动提供帮助。实验表明，ProACT在模拟多人协作任务中显著提升了团队效率和满意度，将智能体从被动助手转变为主动参与者。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 对话智能体在多用户协作中通常被动响应，无法像人类一样主动干预。ProACT基于常识沟通理论，构建故障感知代理框架，能够识别团队中的分歧、模糊目标、遗忘约束等障碍，并在适当时机主动提供帮助。实验表明，ProACT在模拟多人协作任务中显著提升了团队效率和满意度，将智能体从被动助手转变为主动参与者。

quick_skim.title: AgoraSim: A Hybrid Agent-Based Modeling Framework
quick_skim.abstract: LLM-agent simulations make natural-language social scenarios easy to instantiate, but their outputs can be overread as predictions and are often difficult to compare with explicit social dynamics. We present AgoraSim, a hybrid agent-based modeling framework for scenario-oriented social reaction analysis. AgoraSim resolves textual or multimodal artifacts into editable ABM configurations, runs ratio-controlled populations that mix LLM, vision-language, custom-endpoint, random, and classical agents, and compares the same scenario against matched classical reference dynamics. All agents emit a shared structured decision object, enabling common action spaces, interaction protocols, metrics, and audit records. Exposed through a local UI, Python SDK/CLI, and REST API, AgoraSim helps users inspect scenario trajectories, compare modeling assumptions, and identify cases that warrant empirical validation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hybrid agent-based modeling for interaction dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 混合智能体建模框架，模拟交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 混合智能体建模框架，模拟交互动力学
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 混合智能体建模框架，模拟交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgoraSim is a hybrid agent-based modeling framework for social interaction analysis, supporting mixed LLM and classical agents with shared decision objects.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM智能体仿真虽易实例化，但输出常被过度解读且难以与经典动力对比。AgoraSim提供混合智能体建模框架，将文本或模态制品解析为可编辑配置，运行包含LLM、视觉语言、自定义端点、随机和经典智能体的比例控制种群，并与匹配的经典参考动力学对比。所有智能体共享结构化决策对象，支持通用动作空间、交互协议和审计记录。该框架通过本地UI、Python SDK和REST API暴露，服务于场景导向的社会反应分析。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM智能体仿真虽易实例化，但输出常被过度解读且难以与经典动力对比。AgoraSim提供混合智能体建模框架，将文本或模态制品解析为可编辑配置，运行包含LLM、视觉语言、自定义端点、随机和经典智能体的比例控制种群，并与匹配的经典参考动力学对比。所有智能体共享结构化决策对象，支持通用动作空间、交互协议和审计记录。该框架通过本地UI、Python SDK和REST API暴露，服务于场景导向的社会反应分析。

quick_skim.title: Beyond the Leaderboard: A Synthesis of Tool-Use, Planning, and Reasoning Failures in Large Language Model Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly evaluated on their ability to use tools, plan multi-step tasks, coordinate with other agents, and operate over extended horizons. Reported benchmark gains often obscure recurring failure modes documented across otherwise unrelated evaluation efforts. This paper synthesizes 27 benchmark, taxonomy, and audit papers (2023-2026), spanning 19 distinct benchmarks, into a cross-cutting taxonomy of agent limitations. To our knowledge, this is the first synthesis that integrates evidence across tool use, planning, long-horizon reasoning, multi-agent coordination, safety, and measurement validity into a single, unified taxonomy of LLM agent limitations. We identify six failure clusters: (1) tool invocation and parameter-level errors, (2) planning and constraint-satisfaction failures, (3) long-horizon degradation from context accumulation, (4) multi-agent coordination failures, (5) safety and security failures under adversarial or underspecified conditions, and (6) measurement validity problems. The taxonomy was derived iteratively by grouping independently reported error categories into themes corresponding to distinct stages of the agent reasoning-to-action pipeline. Across the literature, we find that failures compound nonlinearly with task length, that strong performance on individual sub-tasks does not reliably translate into end-to-end success, and that additional scaffolding does not consistently improve reliability. At the same time, substantial progress has been demonstrated in single-turn tool use, short-horizon web navigation, and narrowly scoped coding tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: synthesizes multi-agent coordination failures across benchmarks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 综合了跨基准的多代理协调失败模式
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 综合了跨基准的多代理协调失败模式
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 综合了跨基准的多代理协调失败模式
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Synthesizes 27 papers into a unified taxonomy of LLM agent limitations including multi-agent coordination, tool use, planning, and reasoning failures.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM代理的基准评估往往掩盖了反复出现的失败模式。本文综合27篇论文，提出统一的代理局限分类法，涵盖工具使用、规划、推理和多代理协调等六大类失败。该分类法为理解和改进多代理系统提供了系统化的参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM代理的基准评估往往掩盖了反复出现的失败模式。本文综合27篇论文，提出统一的代理局限分类法，涵盖工具使用、规划、推理和多代理协调等六大类失败。该分类法为理解和改进多代理系统提供了系统化的参考。

quick_skim.title: Collective Intelligence with Foundation Models
quick_skim.abstract: As foundation models grow in scale and diversity, coordinating multiple models into cooperative reasoning systems offers a path toward safer, more reliable AI. This chapter presents a multi-agent framework where solver models generate independent drafts, each undergoes structured critique and revision by a critic agent, and an aggregator agent synthesizes a final consensus solution. A scoring module provides semantic, numerical, and procedural evaluation across all agents. Through ablation studies on a benchmark spanning calculus, physics, chemistry, biology, economics, optimization, statistics, and mathematics, we isolate the contributions of framework architecture versus model diversity. We compare four configurations: (1) Individual Baseline, (2) Homogeneous Framework using one shared model, (3) Redundant Homogeneous Solvers using multiple instances of the same model, and (4) Heterogeneous Framework with diverse specialized models. Results show that while framework structure and redundant sampling yield modest gains, model heterogeneity is the critical factor driving substantial performance improvements. The heterogeneous configuration achieves superior step-wise accuracy (0.64 vs. 0.54 for individual models; 2.3x improvement over homogeneous configurations) with reduced variance across categories and difficulty levels. Step-wise reasoning quality (correctness of intermediate steps, not just final answers) improves dramatically only with model diversity, showing that heterogeneous agents provide complementary error detection and reasoning refinement essential for explainability and auditability. We discuss architectural principles, evaluation methodology, and implications for Global Applied AI, showing how heterogeneous multi-agent coordination supports transparent, auditable, high-confidence decision-making across scientific and industrial domains.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent cooperative reasoning framework with critic and aggregator agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 包含批评者和聚合者的多代理协作推理框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 包含批评者和聚合者的多代理协作推理框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 包含批评者和聚合者的多代理协作推理框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a multi-agent framework where models generate drafts, critique, and aggregate for consensus, showing architecture contribution independent of model diversity.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 基础模型在规模增加时可能带来风险，协调多个模型进行协作推理是更可靠的路径。本文提出一个多代理框架：求解器生成独立草稿，批评者进行结构化评审和修订，聚合者合成最终共识。在涵盖多个学科的基准上通过消融实验表明，框架架构本身的贡献独立于模型多样性。该方法提升了推理的安全性和可靠性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 基础模型在规模增加时可能带来风险，协调多个模型进行协作推理是更可靠的路径。本文提出一个多代理框架：求解器生成独立草稿，批评者进行结构化评审和修订，聚合者合成最终共识。在涵盖多个学科的基准上通过消融实验表明，框架架构本身的贡献独立于模型多样性。该方法提升了推理的安全性和可靠性。

quick_skim.title: StateFuse: Deterministic Conflict-Preserving Memory for Multi-Agent Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Agent systems accumulate conflicting observations across branches, retries, and replicas, yet many practical memory layers still collapse disagreement behind overwrite rules that are difficult to inspect or correct. We present StateFuse, a conflict-aware replicated memory contract built on standard OpSet/CRDT merge. StateFuse does not introduce a new join algebra; it defines an agent-facing semantics layer with immutable history, explicit conflict objects, exact and semantic correction handles (claim_id / claim_ref), deterministic predicate contracts, and projection-time resolution that cannot rewrite replicated state.   We evaluate StateFuse against flat multi-value, raw-log, provenance-style, and collapsed baselines under matched resolver and verification policies. On a 282-question official conflict-bearing MemoryAgentBench slice, the compared methods tie on answer accuracy, but conflict-preserving surfaces keep contradictions visible while collapsed surfaces do not. In a controlled agent loop with uniform verification, preserving ambiguity enables safer abstention and correction than early collapse. A correction-handle ablation further shows that semantic handles matter when exact prior identifiers are unavailable.   The resulting claim is narrow: StateFuse is best supported as a safer public memory contract for contradiction surfacing, abstention, and auditable correction, not as a universal accuracy gain.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent conflict-preserving memory for interaction dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体冲突保存内存，支持交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体冲突保存内存，支持交互动力学
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体冲突保存内存，支持交互动力学
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: StateFuse provides deterministic conflict-aware memory for multi-agent systems, preserving disagreement via CRDT-based semantics, enabling robust interaction dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体系统在分支、重试和副本间积累冲突观测，但现有内存层常以难以检查的覆写规则掩盖分歧。StateFuse提出基于OpSet/CRDT合并的冲突感知复制内存合约，通过不可变历史、显式冲突对象和确定性谓词合约保留交互细节。实验表明，StateFuse在保持状态一致性的同时，提供了比平多值、原始日志等基线更高的可审计性和可修正性，为多智能体交互动态提供可靠的基础设施。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多智能体系统在分支、重试和副本间积累冲突观测，但现有内存层常以难以检查的覆写规则掩盖分歧。StateFuse提出基于OpSet/CRDT合并的冲突感知复制内存合约，通过不可变历史、显式冲突对象和确定性谓词合约保留交互细节。实验表明，StateFuse在保持状态一致性的同时，提供了比平多值、原始日志等基线更高的可审计性和可修正性，为多智能体交互动态提供可靠的基础设施。
